content.question: Do the short-term test-score gains in selective tracks reflect genuine value-added skill formation or primarily a reallocation of measured achievement away from non-selected peers, and if genuine, why do these gains fade out in tertiary and labor-market outcomes?
content.reduction_path: Extend the Gurzó and Horn longitudinal datasets to include (a) direct measurement of post-secondary educational attainment and earnings by 10+ years after secondary school, (b) control for selection into higher-education program types, and (c) robustness checks for measurement timing and sample attrition; separately, conduct a skill-content analysis of exam questions to assess whether gains reflect curriculum-specific content or broader reasoning ability.
